frameworks.name: factorised representations
frameworks.one_line: EC factorises experience into transferable dimensions for cross-context generalisation

frameworks.name: graph-structured task representation
frameworks.one_line: behavioural sequences governed by underlying transition graph between locations

frameworks.name: representational similarity analysis (fMRI)
frameworks.one_line: neural pattern similarity used to index shared generalisable structure

core_question: Does the entorhinal cortex represent which dimensions of past experience can be generalised to a novel environment?
key_assumptions: experience can be decomposed into separable, transferable dimensions
shared graph transitions support generalisation across environments
neural similarity in EC indexes which features are treated as common
manipulations: whether sequence transitions are shared or differ across environments
early vs later exposure to the novel environment
key_findings: accuracy higher on blocks with shared transitions across environments
generalisation advantage strongest during early novel-environment exposure
EC patterns showed greater similarity for generalisable starting locations across environments
interpretations: EC supports generalisation by forming factorised representations isolating transferable features
neural differentiation of generalisable aspects underlies the behavioural transfer benefit
